subject: Ecology
title: Hawaii
description: A quick, practical guide to Hawaii.

hook.text: Hawaii is the most isolated archipelago on Earth—3,800 km from the nearest continent.

buildup.text: Only about 750 founding species ever reached Hawaii naturally. They produced over 10,000.

discovery.text: A single tarweed ancestor from California radiated into the silversword alliance—28 species.

twist.text: Since human arrival, Hawaii has lost more species than any other US state—by a wide margin.

climax.text: Invasive species, disease, and habitat loss continue to threaten Hawaii's remaining endemics.

punchline.text: The most remote islands created the most unique life.

quiz.question: How many founding species naturally reached Hawaii?
